notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:1463:+Define the smallest practical review gate between one prompt run finishing and the next prompt in sequence being treated as ready.
notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:1469:+- a conservative human stop before queue progression
notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:1486:+- a larger queue state machine
notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:1532:+- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:1544:+- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:1546:+- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:1548:+`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:1549:+`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:1553:+`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:1564:+V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
notes/006_define_v1_run_review_gate__SUCCESS__20260415_203019.md:1594:+The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.

notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:5:The V1 prompt workflow is now real, not just specified. The repo has a working runner at `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, an in-place review write-back helper at `tools/codex/review_run.py`, a queue-readiness checker at `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py`, and a review-backlog lister at `tools/codex/list_review_backlog.py`.
notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:13:- One bounded prompt run can be executed through `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`.
notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:17:- Conservative queue progression is operational through `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py`.
notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:31:  - It still targets `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`.
notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:32:  - It still names the runner field as `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`.
notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:33:  - It still frames review write-back and queue logic as not yet implemented, while the repo now has separate helpers for both.
notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:34:- The execution-record spec says the stable V1 `run_id` is `<prompt_stem>__<started_at_utc>`, but `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` now adds same-second suffixes like `__2` when needed. The code is pragmatic; the spec lags.
notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:42:The repo has outgrown parts of the design packet. The next prompt or reviewer could still be steered by stale references to `baby_run_prompt.py`, by the old strict run-id rule, or by the older assumption that readiness/backlog support does not exist.
notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:52:`run_prompt.py`, `review_run.py`, `check_queue_readiness.py`, and `list_review_backlog.py` all rely on the same markdown field and section conventions. That contract is now central to the workflow, but it is only protected by careful coding and prior notes, not by a small repeatable test layer.
notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:55:- repeated required-section and required-field parsing logic in `review_run.py`, `check_queue_readiness.py`, and `list_review_backlog.py`
notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:65:That is not a V1 architecture failure, but it does create friction when interpreting readiness and when deciding whether to trust the queue as a day-to-day operational surface.
notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:68:- `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py` needed a special explanatory `Queue note:`
notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:71:### 4. Defer retry-linkage tooling and richer queue semantics
notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:100:4. `Retry-linkage helper or richer queue semantics`
notes/018_architecture_vs_actual_sweep__20260416_005130.md:117:- update the bridge-runner spec to reflect `run_prompt.py`
notes/012_v1_pipeline_options_review__20260416_000819.md:9:- execute one prompt through `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`
notes/012_v1_pipeline_options_review__20260416_000819.md:20:- Prompt resolution exists and is conservative. `run_prompt.py` preserves the same direct-path, `codex_prompts/`, and unique-prefix lookup behavior as the bridge runner baseline. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:31).
notes/012_v1_pipeline_options_review__20260416_000819.md:21:- Codex invocation is still thin and inspectable. The runner shells out through `codex exec -C <repo_root> --output-last-message <tempfile> -` and captures return code, final output, and stderr. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:74).
notes/012_v1_pipeline_options_review__20260416_000819.md:22:- Execution-record creation exists and matches the intended V1 section order. The runner writes header, execution facts, review facts, failure analysis, resource facts, prompt text, final output, and stderr into one markdown file. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:97).
notes/012_v1_pipeline_options_review__20260416_000819.md:23:- Resource and failure evidence capture exists in the actual record body. The runner records `return_code`, `elapsed_seconds`, output/stderr character counts, and full stderr text when present. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:144) and the failed record [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md:8).
notes/012_v1_pipeline_options_review__20260416_000819.md:24:- The separation between execution outcome and acceptance outcome is implemented correctly. New records start at `review_status: UNREVIEWED` regardless of `EXECUTED` vs `EXECUTION_FAILED`. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:130).
notes/012_v1_pipeline_options_review__20260416_000819.md:31:- The helper is consistent with the V1 review gate. It only allows `ACCEPTED` or `REJECTED`, auto-fills `reviewed_at_utc`, and limits failure-analysis updates to rejected runs. See [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:12) and [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:148).
notes/012_v1_pipeline_options_review__20260416_000819.md:47:### 1. Conservative queue progression is still architectural, not operational
notes/012_v1_pipeline_options_review__20260416_000819.md:69:The execution-record artifact says the stable V1 `run_id` is `<prompt_stem>__<started_at_utc>`, but `run_prompt.py` adds `__2`, `__3`, etc. on same-second collisions. See [V1_Execution_Record_Artifact.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Execution_Record_Artifact.md:37) versus [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:59).
notes/012_v1_pipeline_options_review__20260416_000819.md:75:- `tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md` still targets `baby_run_prompt.py` as the implementation target and still names the runner as `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`. See [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:5), [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:17), [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:156), and [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:252).
notes/012_v1_pipeline_options_review__20260416_000819.md:76:- The design packet now implicitly assumes a review write-back step, but the architecture document still describes conservative queue progression as part of the V1 shape without a concrete implementation artifact for that responsibility. See [Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:161) and [Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:215).
notes/012_v1_pipeline_options_review__20260416_000819.md:103:- implementation target naming drift: the bridge-runner spec still speaks in terms of `baby_run_prompt.py`, while the actual V1 runner is `run_prompt.py`

notes/012_v1_pipeline_options_review__20260416_000819.md:111:### Option 1. Add a queue-readiness checker

notes/002_repo_inventory_and_status__SUCCESS__20260415_133347.md:32:The repository looks mature at its core: the main README, install docs, environment files, Docker setup, chapter notebooks, and image assets are all present and coherent.
notes/002_repo_inventory_and_status__SUCCESS__20260415_133347.md:36:- Main project documentation is clear and points to notebook-first usage.
notes/002_repo_inventory_and_status__SUCCESS__20260415_133347.md:38:- The repo is notebook-heavy; most work appears to happen in Jupyter rather than packaged Python modules.

notes/002_repo_inventory_and_status__SUCCESS__20260415_133347.md:53:Overall status: usable and substantial, with a strong mature upstream notebook base, but locally extended in a way that now needs clearer boundaries and documentation. The most obvious attention areas are documenting the weather subproject and Codex workflow pieces, clarifying the intended entry points, and adding at least lightweight automated validation for the local tooling.

notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:645:### 4. V1 review gate definition
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:659:No additional required queue file, database, or sidecar should be introduced in V1.
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:677:* a generalized queue engine
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:715:The review gate is the minimum manual checkpoint after execution record creation.
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:726:This means the review gate is not a separate platform subsystem.
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:737:The review gate defined in `tools/codex/V1_Run_Review_Gate.md` is therefore part of the architecture boundary for V1, not an optional later add-on.
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:813:`005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__20260415_202557`
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:820:- it can be created cheaply by the bridge runner
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1252:Define the smallest practical review gate between one prompt run finishing and the next prompt in sequence being treated as ready.
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1258:- a conservative human stop before queue progression
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1275:- a larger queue state machine
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1321:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1333:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1335:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1337:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1338:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1342:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1353:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1383:The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1390:- automatic queue release
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1392:- separate queue-level state files
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1400:The minimum V1 review gate is:
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1413:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,260p' tools/codex/run_prompt.py" in /data/git/handson-ml3
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1430:RUNNER_PATH = "tools/codex/run_prompt.py"
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1855:- execute one prompt through `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1866:- Prompt resolution exists and is conservative. `run_prompt.py` preserves the same direct-path, `codex_prompts/`, and unique-prefix lookup behavior as the bridge runner baseline. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:31).
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1867:- Codex invocation is still thin and inspectable. The runner shells out through `codex exec -C <repo_root> --output-last-message <tempfile> -` and captures return code, final output, and stderr. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:74).
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1868:- Execution-record creation exists and matches the intended V1 section order. The runner writes header, execution facts, review facts, failure analysis, resource facts, prompt text, final output, and stderr into one markdown file. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:97).
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1869:- Resource and failure evidence capture exists in the actual record body. The runner records `return_code`, `elapsed_seconds`, output/stderr character counts, and full stderr text when present. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:144) and the failed record [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md:8).
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1870:- The separation between execution outcome and acceptance outcome is implemented correctly. New records start at `review_status: UNREVIEWED` regardless of `EXECUTED` vs `EXECUTION_FAILED`. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:130).
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1877:- The helper is consistent with the V1 review gate. It only allows `ACCEPTED` or `REJECTED`, auto-fills `reviewed_at_utc`, and limits failure-analysis updates to rejected runs. See [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:12) and [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:148).
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1893:### 1. Conservative queue progression is still architectural, not operational
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1915:The execution-record artifact says the stable V1 `run_id` is `<prompt_stem>__<started_at_utc>`, but `run_prompt.py` adds `__2`, `__3`, etc. on same-second collisions. notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:2806:The review concludes the V1 pipe is now a real usable thin slice: `run_prompt.py` creates durable execution records, `review_run.py` performs in-place manual review write-back, and the main remaining weakness is operational support for repeated use rather than missing architecture. The top recommendation is to add a small queue-readiness checker next, followed by an unreviewed-run lister and then lightweight contract validation for the two V1 scripts.
notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:2808:I did not modify `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`, `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, or `tools/codex/review_run.py`.

notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:2988:The prompts are bounded, implementation-ready, and explicitly constrained against broader queue-engine or dashboard growth. The plan note explains why these two were chosen, why readiness checking comes first, and what should wait. I did not implement either helper and did not modify the protected runner/review files.

notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:3056:I’ve pinned down the shape: the helper can stay single-file by parsing a handful of markdown field lines, treating only V1 execution records as candidates, and defaulting to the earliest queue target released by the current accepted-prefix evidence. I’m writing the checker now, then I’ll run a small validation pass and capture the result in the implementation note.

notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:70:- `notes/009_run_prompt_candidate_build__20260415_233407.md`
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:71:- `notes/010_run_prompt_candidate_review__20260415_234559.md`

notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:200:The review concludes the V1 pipe is now a real usable thin slice: `run_prompt.py` creates durable execution records, `review_run.py` performs in-place manual review write-back, and the main remaining weakness is operational support for repeated use rather than missing architecture. The top recommendation is to add a small queue-readiness checker next, followed by an unreviewed-run lister and then lightweight contract validation for the two V1 scripts.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:202:I did not modify `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`, `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, or `tools/codex/review_run.py`.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:232:Do NOT modify `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`, `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, or `tools/codex/review_run.py`.

notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:657:- it can be created cheaply by the bridge runner
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:906:* one manual review gate
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:936:* stopping the queue at `UNREVIEWED`
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:949:* richer queue state machines
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:999:### 4. V1 review gate definition
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1013:No additional required queue file, database, or sidecar should be introduced in V1.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1031:* a generalized queue engine
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1069:The review gate is the minimum manual checkpoint after execution record creation.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1080:This means the review gate is not a separate platform subsystem.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1091:The review gate defined in `tools/codex/V1_Run_Review_Gate.md` is therefore part of the architecture boundary for V1, not an optional later add-on.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1127:Define the smallest practical review gate between one prompt run finishing and the next prompt in sequence being treated as ready.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1133:- a conservative human stop before queue progression
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1150:- a larger queue state machine
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1196:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1208:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1210:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1212:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1213:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1217:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1228:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1258:The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1265:- automatic queue release
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1267:- separate queue-level state files
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1275:The minimum V1 review gate is:
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1292:Define the smallest change set for [baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py) so the current bridge runner writes a V1 execution record in `notes/` without a broader refactor.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1304:This spec covers only the current bridge runner at [baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py).
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1329:The current gap is that the note conflates execution completion with outcome labeling and does not initialize the V1 review gate.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1404:No broader token accounting, file-diff analysis, validation harvesting, or queue-state calculation should be added in this pass.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1412:- queue progression logic beyond initializing `review_status: UNREVIEWED`
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1443:- runner: `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1539:- `runner` -> literal `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1552:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,260p' tools/codex/run_prompt.py" in /data/git/handson-ml3
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1569:RUNNER_PATH = "tools/codex/run_prompt.py"
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:1984:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,260p' tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py" in /data/git/handson-ml3
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:2140:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,240p' notes/009_run_prompt_candidate_build__20260415_233407.md" in /data/git/handson-ml3
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:2146:Built `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` as a thin V1 runner candidate that resolves one prompt, runs `codex exec` from the repo root, captures `--output-last-message`, captures stderr and return code, writes one V1 execution-record markdown file into `notes/`, prints that path, and exits with the subprocess return code.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:2148:## Preserved From `baby_run_prompt.py`
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:2172:- Updated the runner identity field to `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:2200:- `python3 tools/codex/run_prompt.py codex_prompts/001_smoke_test_pipeline.md`
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:2209:- `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` remained unchanged. `git diff -- tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` returned no diff.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:2217:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,240p' notes/010_run_prompt_candidate_review__20260415_234559.md" in /data/git/handson-ml3
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:2223:`tools/codex/run_prompt.py` appears to satisfy the intended V1 bridge-runner role: it executes one prompt, writes one durable execution record in `notes/`, separates execution from review, and stops at `review_status: UNREVIEWED`. The sample record is structurally close to the V1 artifact shape and preserves the failure evidence that matters. The most important next issue is environment-related, not runner design or broader implementation.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:2227:### Does `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` successfully implement the V1 design intent?
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:2239:- no queue automation or review automation
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:2243:- It stays thin and preserves the bounded behavior of `baby_run_prompt.py` instead of turning into a larger system.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:2254:- The runner identity now correctly points at `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, but `tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md` still describes the prior target as `baby_run_prompt.py`. That is a doc drift issue more than a runner issue.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:2325:- Nothing in `run_prompt.py` suggests it is forcing a read-only mode or attempting forbidden writes beyond the existing thin subprocess path.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:2364:A short diagnosis artifact that identifies where session startup is trying to write, whether the failure reproduces independently of `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, and one bounded follow-up action.

notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:3346:### 5. Conservative queue progression
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:3358:The implementation order should follow the governing workflow and the queued design decisions:
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:3360:1. define the V1 review gate
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:3361:2. align the architecture document to the workflow, the execution record, and the review gate
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:3371:* coupling queue progression to process exit
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:3416:### Richer status and queue models
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:3440:* aggregated queue reports
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:3462:2. keep the review gate explicit

notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:3605:I’ve identified the main implementation seams already: one is documentation drift around the runner target and identity rules, and another is that the review gate exists operationally but not yet as an explicit queue-release mechanism. I’m pulling exact line references now so the report can stay grounded in the repo rather than general commentary.

notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4719:+- execute one prompt through `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4730:+- Prompt resolution exists and is conservative. `run_prompt.py` preserves the same direct-path, `codex_prompts/`, and unique-prefix lookup behavior as the bridge runner baseline. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:31).
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4731:+- Codex invocation is still thin and inspectable. The runner shells out through `codex exec -C <repo_root> --output-last-message <tempfile> -` and captures return code, final output, and stderr. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:74).
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4732:+- Execution-record creation exists and matches the intended V1 section order. The runner writes header, execution facts, review facts, failure analysis, resource facts, prompt text, final output, and stderr into one markdown file. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:97).
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4733:+- Resource and failure evidence capture exists in the actual record body. The runner records `return_code`, `elapsed_seconds`, output/stderr character counts, and full stderr text when present. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:144) and the failed record [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md:8).
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4734:+- The separation between execution outcome and acceptance outcome is implemented correctly. New records start at `review_status: UNREVIEWED` regardless of `EXECUTED` vs `EXECUTION_FAILED`. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:130).
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:4741:+- The helper is consistent with the V1 review gate. It only allows `ACCEPTED` or `REJECTED`, auto-fills `reviewed_at_utc`, and limits failure-analysis updates to rejected runs. See [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:12) and [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:148).

notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5241:+The execution-record artifact says the stable V1 `run_id` is `<prompt_stem>__<started_at_utc>`, but `run_prompt.py` adds `__2`, `__3`, etc. on same-second collisions. See [V1_Execution_Record_Artifact.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Execution_Record_Artifact.md:37) versus [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:59).
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5247:+- `tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md` still targets `baby_run_prompt.py` as the implementation target and still names the runner as `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`. See [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:5), [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:17), [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:156), and [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:252).
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5248:+- The design packet now implicitly assumes a review write-back step, but the architecture document still describes conservative queue progression as part of the V1 shape without a concrete implementation artifact for that responsibility. See [Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:161) and [Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:215).
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5275:+- implementation target naming drift: the bridge-runner spec still speaks in terms of `baby_run_prompt.py`, while the actual V1 runner is `run_prompt.py`
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5276:+- runner identity drift: the spec examples still encode `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` while the real records encode `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5283:+### Option 1. Add a queue-readiness checker
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5306:+  - A small test or fixture harness around `run_prompt.py` and `review_run.py` that locks in the record shape, allowed review transitions, and in-place write-back behavior.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5316:+  - A cleanup pass on the architecture/spec packet so it reflects `run_prompt.py`, the existing review write-back helper, and the real collision rule.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5372:+### 1. Add a queue-readiness checker
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5388:+### 3. Add lightweight contract validation for `run_prompt.py` and `review_run.py`

notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5426:+1. queue-readiness checker
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5445:+- execute one prompt through `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5456:+- Prompt resolution exists and is conservative. `run_prompt.py` preserves the same direct-path, `codex_prompts/`, and unique-prefix lookup behavior as the bridge runner baseline. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:31).
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5457:+- Codex invocation is still thin and inspectable. The runner shells out through `codex exec -C <repo_root> --output-last-message <tempfile> -` and captures return code, final output, and stderr. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:74).
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5458:+- Execution-record creation exists and matches the intended V1 section order. The runner writes header, execution facts, review facts, failure analysis, resource facts, prompt text, final output, and stderr into one markdown file. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:97).
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5459:+- Resource and failure evidence capture exists in the actual record body. The runner records `return_code`, `elapsed_seconds`, output/stderr character counts, and full stderr text when present. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:144) and the failed record [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md:8).
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5460:+- The separation between execution outcome and acceptance outcome is implemented correctly. New records start at `review_status: UNREVIEWED` regardless of `EXECUTED` vs `EXECUTION_FAILED`. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:130).
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:5467:+- The helper is consistent with the V1 review gate. It only allows `ACCEPTED` or `REJECTED`, auto-fills `reviewed_at_utc`, and limits failure-analysis updates to rejected runs. See [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:12) and [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:148).

See [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:12) and [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:148).

See [V1_Execution_Record_Artifact.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Execution_Record_Artifact.md:37) versus [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:59).

See [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:5), [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:17), [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:156), and [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:252).

See [Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:161) and [Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:215).

notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:6338:+- runner identity drift: the spec examples still encode `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` while the real records encode `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:6345:+### Option 1. Add a queue-readiness checker
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:6368:+  - A small test or fixture harness around `run_prompt.py` and `review_run.py` that locks in the record shape, allowed review transitions, and in-place write-back behavior.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:6378:+  - A cleanup pass on the architecture/spec packet so it reflects `run_prompt.py`, the existing review write-back helper, and the real collision rule.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:6430:The review concludes the V1 pipe is now a real usable thin slice: `run_prompt.py` creates durable execution records, `review_run.py` performs in-place manual review write-back, and the main remaining weakness is operational support for repeated use rather than missing architecture. The top recommendation is to add a small queue-readiness checker next, followed by an unreviewed-run lister and then lightweight contract validation for the two V1 scripts.
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:6432:I did not modify `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`, `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, or `tools/codex/review_run.py`.

notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:6527:+- Prompt resolution exists and is conservative. `run_prompt.py` preserves the same direct-path, `codex_prompts/`, and unique-prefix lookup behavior as the bridge runner baseline. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:31).
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:6528:+- Codex invocation is still thin and inspectable. The runner shells out through `codex exec -C <repo_root> --output-last-message <tempfile> -` and captures return code, final output, and stderr. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:74).
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:6529:+- Execution-record creation exists and matches the intended V1 section order. The runner writes header, execution facts, review facts, failure analysis, resource facts, prompt text, final output, and stderr into one markdown file. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:97).
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:6530:+- Resource and failure evidence capture exists in the actual record body. The runner records `return_code`, `elapsed_seconds`, output/stderr character counts, and full stderr text when present. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:144) and the failed record [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md:8).
notes/012_review_v1_pipeline_and_recommend_next_options__20260416_000658.md:6531:+- The separation between execution outcome and acceptance outcome is implemented correctly. New records start at `review_status: UNREVIEWED` regardless of `EXECUTED` vs `EXECUTION_FAILED`. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:130).

notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:998:`tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md` is directionally aligned with the governing workflow because it pushes toward smaller parts, clearer ownership, and conservative growth. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` is acceptable as thin bridge tooling for a single bounded slice, but the current architecture doc does not yet translate the workflow into a clear V1 boundary, validation plan, review loop, or failure/resource evidence model. The next step should stay at the design layer: define the minimum execution-record artifact that every run must produce before more tooling is added.
notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1019:- The runner does one bounded thing: resolve a prompt, execute `codex exec`, and write a local note. That fits the workflow's allowance for thin bridge tooling that gathers evidence while remaining inspectable and reversible ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:114), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:99)).
notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1020:- It validates basic directory prerequisites and fails cleanly when expected folders or prompt files are missing, which is a reasonable minimal safeguard for a bridge script ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:109), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:108)).
notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1021:- It writes timestamped markdown notes into `notes/`, which gives the system at least some durable local history of what was run and what Codex returned ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:120), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:46), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:135)).
notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1025:- Prompt resolution policy is embedded in the runner. Selection by absolute path, repo-relative path, `codex_prompts/` path, or globbed prefix is helpful now, but prompt identity and lookup rules belong in a prompt-discovery/status layer if the system grows ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:47), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:25)).
notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1026:- Note-path and note-content policy are also embedded here. That is acceptable for a bridge script, but note schema, status vocabulary, and execution-record structure should eventually be owned by a note/history module rather than the process launcher ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:96), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:187), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:50)).
notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1027:- The runner currently decides final state as `SUCCESS` or `FAILED` based only on the subprocess exit code. That is an expedient bridge behavior, but review judgment, validation outcome, and richer state modeling should not stay inside the launcher if the workflow matures ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:141), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:124)).
notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1031:- As bridge tooling it is still acceptable, but it is only partially subordinate to workflow. It captures execution, yet it does not encode the review point that should separate execution from acceptance. A zero exit code becomes `SUCCESS` immediately, which skips the workflow's "validate and inspect before the next step" posture ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:271), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:125)).
notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1032:- It stores the original prompt text and final output, but not explicit success criteria, validation evidence, or reviewer judgment. That means the note is an execution artifact, not yet a full workflow artifact ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:251), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).
notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1036:- The current setup provides some raw failure evidence because nonzero exit runs still write a note and stderr is preserved when present ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:343), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:128)).
notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1042:- The setup preserves only minimal cost evidence today: timestamp in the note filename/content and the captured final output/stderr. That gives some weak signal about artifact volume, but almost nothing about runtime, retries, or service usage ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:382), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:17), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).
notes/005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__SUCCESS__20260415_202522.md:1085:I have the governing constraints and the main seam: the current bridge runner records execution, but it conflates execution success with reviewed acceptance and leaves the run artifact underdefined. I’m checking the existing prompt queue so the next sequence stays small and fits the repository’s current naming pattern.

notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:460:I’m inspecting the specified workflow docs, runner files, and sample notes first so the helper matches the existing V1 record shape and review gate without touching the runners.
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:514:* one manual review gate
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:544:* stopping the queue at `UNREVIEWED`
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:557:* richer queue state machines
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:607:### 4. V1 review gate definition
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:621:No additional required queue file, database, or sidecar should be introduced in V1.
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:639:* a generalized queue engine
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:677:The review gate is the minimum manual checkpoint after execution record creation.
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:688:This means the review gate is not a separate platform subsystem.
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:699:The review gate defined in `tools/codex/V1_Run_Review_Gate.md` is therefore part of the architecture boundary for V1, not an optional later add-on.
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:775:`005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__20260415_202557`
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:782:- it can be created cheaply by the bridge runner
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1214:Define the smallest practical review gate between one prompt run finishing and the next prompt in sequence being treated as ready.
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1220:- a conservative human stop before queue progression
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1237:- a larger queue state machine
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1283:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1295:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1297:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1299:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1300:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1304:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1315:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1345:The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1352:- automatic queue release
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1354:- separate queue-level state files
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:1362:The minimum V1 review gate is:

notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:2028:Built `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` as a thin V1 runner candidate that resolves one prompt, runs `codex exec` from the repo root, captures `--output-last-message`, captures stderr and return code, writes one V1 execution-record markdown file into `notes/`, prints that path, and exits with the subprocess return code.

notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:2099:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,260p' notes/010_run_prompt_candidate_review__20260415_234559.md" in /data/git/handson-ml3
notes/011_build_v1_review_writeback_helper__20260415_235346.md:2105:`tools/codex/run_prompt.py` appears to satisfy the intended V1 bridge-runner role: it executes one prompt, writes one durable execution record in `notes/`, separates execution from review, and stops at `review_status: UNREVIEWED`. The sample record is structurally close to the V1 artifact shape and preserves the failure evidence that matters. The most important next issue is environment-related, not runner design or broader implementation.

notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:21:Zero Goal Before / Implementation Notes cells. The 60 non-empty code cells have no pedagogical treatment whatsoever. What exists is purely the original Géron narrative — section headings, brief book-prose intros to subsections, and exercise solutions with conversational narration — none of it in the Goal/Implementation format.
notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:25:Cell 0 is just `**Support Vector Machines**` — 27 characters. There is no chapter-level intro in the treatment format. Not even close.
notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:29:The entire notebook is untreated. Every code cell from cell 5 through cell 112 (60 code cells) lacks both a Goal Before and an Implementation Notes After cell. This isn't a gap — it's a blank canvas.
notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:33:This notebook needs a complete treatment pass from scratch. Significant work.
notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:41:All 58 non-empty code cells have both a "### Goal Before This Cell" and a "### Implementation Notes After This Cell" cell. The treatment is thorough, consistent in format, and well-written throughout. The 5% gap is one thing: the chapter-level intro.
notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:45:Cell 0: `**Chapter 6 – Decision Trees**` — 30 characters. Just a bold heading. There is no chapter-level intro in the treatment format — none at all. This is the single missing piece.
notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:49:No untreated code cell stretches. The only gap is the chapter-level intro markdown cell that should appear near the top of the notebook before the Setup section. Everything else is done.
notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:61:Only 17 of 73 non-empty code cells have both a Goal + Implementation treatment. The notebook uses a different and inconsistent treatment vocabulary — cells say "**Why run this cell**", "**Result**:", "**What it is**" instead of the canonical "### Goal Before This Cell" / "### Implementation Notes After This Cell" format. So even treated cells may not fully match the template.
notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:73:Substantial work remaining. The treatment that exists uses a looser format than the canonical template. A full pass would need to both add missing cells AND potentially revise existing partial cells to use the standard structure.
notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:81:NB07 is second priority — has a foundation but needs a major treatment pass.  
notes/001_notebook_triage_05_06_07.md:82:NB05 should be scheduled last — needs a full treatment from scratch.

notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:199:  → Read the specified workflow, architecture, V1 record, review gate, runner, and note files
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:476:* one manual review gate
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:506:* stopping the queue at `UNREVIEWED`
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:519:* richer queue state machines
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:569:### 4. V1 review gate definition
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:583:No additional required queue file, database, or sidecar should be introduced in V1.
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:601:* a generalized queue engine
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:639:The review gate is the minimum manual checkpoint after execution record creation.
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:650:This means the review gate is not a separate platform subsystem.
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:661:The review gate defined in `tools/codex/V1_Run_Review_Gate.md` is therefore part of the architecture boundary for V1, not an optional later add-on.
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:737:`005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__20260415_202557`
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:744:- it can be created cheaply by the bridge runner
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:952:Define the smallest practical review gate between one prompt run finishing and the next prompt in sequence being treated as ready.
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:958:- a conservative human stop before queue progression
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:975:- a larger queue state machine
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1021:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1033:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1035:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1037:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1038:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1042:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1053:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1083:The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1090:- automatic queue release
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1092:- separate queue-level state files
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1100:The minimum V1 review gate is:
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1111:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,260p' tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py" in /data/git/handson-ml3
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1273:`tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md` is directionally aligned with the governing workflow because it pushes toward smaller parts, clearer ownership, and conservative growth. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` is acceptable as thin bridge tooling for a single bounded slice, but the current architecture doc does not yet translate the workflow into a clear V1 boundary, validation plan, review loop, or failure/resource evidence model. The next step should stay at the design layer: define the minimum execution-record artifact that every run must produce before more tooling is added.
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1294:- The runner does one bounded thing: resolve a prompt, execute `codex exec`, and write a local note. That fits the workflow's allowance for thin bridge tooling that gathers evidence while remaining inspectable and reversible ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:114), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:99)).
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1295:- It validates basic directory prerequisites and fails cleanly when expected folders or prompt files are missing, which is a reasonable minimal safeguard for a bridge script ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:109), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:108)).
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1296:- It writes timestamped markdown notes into `notes/`, which gives the system at least some durable local history of what was run and what Codex returned ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:120), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:46), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:135)).
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1300:- Prompt resolution policy is embedded in the runner. Selection by absolute path, repo-relative path, `codex_prompts/` path, or globbed prefix is helpful now, but prompt identity and lookup rules belong in a prompt-discovery/status layer if the system grows ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:47), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:25)).
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1301:- Note-path and note-content policy are also embedded here. That is acceptable for a bridge script, but note schema, status vocabulary, and execution-record structure should eventually be owned by a note/history module rather than the process launcher ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:96), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:187), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:50)).
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1302:- The runner currently decides final state as `SUCCESS` or `FAILED` based only on the subprocess exit code. That is an expedient bridge behavior, but review judgment, validation outcome, and richer state modeling should not stay inside the launcher if the workflow matures ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:141), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:124)).
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1306:- As bridge tooling it is still acceptable, but it is only partially subordinate to workflow. It captures execution, yet it does not encode the review point that should separate execution from acceptance. A zero exit code becomes `SUCCESS` immediately, which skips the workflow's "validate and inspect before the next step" posture ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:271), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:125)).
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1307:- It stores the original prompt text and final output, but not explicit success criteria, validation evidence, or reviewer judgment. That means the note is an execution artifact, not yet a full workflow artifact ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:251), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1311:- The current setup provides some raw failure evidence because nonzero exit runs still write a note and stderr is preserved when present ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:343), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:128)).
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1317:- The setup preserves only minimal cost evidence today: timestamp in the note filename/content and the captured final output/stderr. That gives some weak signal about artifact volume, but almost nothing about runtime, retries, or service usage ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:382), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:17), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1335:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,260p' notes/005_prompt_queue_plan__20260415_202557.md" in /data/git/handson-ml3
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1344:The queue stays small on purpose.
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1351:1. define the review gate
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1355:This reduces the risk of hard-coding the wrong note model or the wrong queue semantics into the runner before the manual review loop is settled.
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1373:The following work is intentionally not in this queue:
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1473:### 5. Conservative queue progression
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1485:The implementation order should follow the governing workflow and the queued design decisions:
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1487:1. define the V1 review gate
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1488:2. align the architecture document to the workflow, the execution record, and the review gate
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1498:* coupling queue progression to process exit
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1543:### Richer status and queue models
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1567:* aggregated queue reports
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1589:2. keep the review gate explicit
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1592:5. defer broader queue features until the single-run slice has been used enough to reveal real needs
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1895:  ✓ Read the specified workflow, architecture, V1 record, review gate, runner, and note files
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1913:+Define the smallest change set for [baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py) so the current bridge runner writes a V1 execution record in `notes/` without a broader refactor.
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1925:+This spec covers only the current bridge runner at [baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py).
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:1950:+The current gap is that the note conflates execution completion with outcome labeling and does not initialize the V1 review gate.
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:2025:+No broader token accounting, file-diff analysis, validation harvesting, or queue-state calculation should be added in this pass.
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:2033:+- queue progression logic beyond initializing `review_status: UNREVIEWED`
notes/008_define_minimal_bridge_runner_change_spec__SUCCESS__20260415_203548.md:2064:+- runner: `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`

notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:158:The prompts are bounded, implementation-ready, and explicitly constrained against broader queue-engine or dashboard growth. The plan note explains why these two were chosen, why readiness checking comes first, and what should wait. I did not implement either helper and did not modify the protected runner/review files.

notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:674:### 4. V1 review gate definition
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:688:No additional required queue file, database, or sidecar should be introduced in V1.
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:706:* a generalized queue engine
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:744:The review gate is the minimum manual checkpoint after execution record creation.
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:755:This means the review gate is not a separate platform subsystem.
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:766:The review gate defined in `tools/codex/V1_Run_Review_Gate.md` is therefore part of the architecture boundary for V1, not an optional later add-on.
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:842:`005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__20260415_202557`
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:849:- it can be created cheaply by the bridge runner
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1057:Define the smallest practical review gate between one prompt run finishing and the next prompt in sequence being treated as ready.
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1063:- a conservative human stop before queue progression
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1080:- a larger queue state machine
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1126:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1138:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1140:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1142:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1143:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1147:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1158:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1188:The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1195:- automatic queue release
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1197:- separate queue-level state files
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1205:The minimum V1 review gate is:

notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1713:- Prompt resolution exists and is conservative. `run_prompt.py` preserves the same direct-path, `codex_prompts/`, and unique-prefix lookup behavior as the bridge runner baseline. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:31).
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1714:- Codex invocation is still thin and inspectable. The runner shells out through `codex exec -C <repo_root> --output-last-message <tempfile> -` and captures return code, final output, and stderr. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:74).
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1715:- Execution-record creation exists and matches the intended V1 section order. The runner writes header, execution facts, review facts, failure analysis, resource facts, prompt text, final output, and stderr into one markdown file. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:97).
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1716:- Resource and failure evidence capture exists in the actual record body. The runner records `return_code`, `elapsed_seconds`, output/stderr character counts, and full stderr text when present. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:144) and the failed record [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md:8).
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1717:- The separation between execution outcome and acceptance outcome is implemented correctly. New records start at `review_status: UNREVIEWED` regardless of `EXECUTED` vs `EXECUTION_FAILED`. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:130).
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1724:- The helper is consistent with the V1 review gate. It only allows `ACCEPTED` or `REJECTED`, auto-fills `reviewed_at_utc`, and limits failure-analysis updates to rejected runs. See [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:12) and [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:148).
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1740:### 1. Conservative queue progression is still architectural, not operational
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1762:The execution-record artifact says the stable V1 `run_id` is `<prompt_stem>__<started_at_utc>`, but `run_prompt.py` adds `__2`, `__3`, etc. on same-second collisions. See [V1_Execution_Record_Artifact.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Execution_Record_Artifact.md:37) versus [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:59).
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1768:- `tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md` still targets `baby_run_prompt.py` as the implementation target and still names the runner as `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`. See [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:5), [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:17), [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:156), and [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:252).
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1769:- The design packet now implicitly assumes a review write-back step, but the architecture document still describes conservative queue progression as part of the V1 shape without a concrete implementation artifact for that responsibility. See [Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:161) and [Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:215).
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1796:- implementation target naming drift: the bridge-runner spec still speaks in terms of `baby_run_prompt.py`, while the actual V1 runner is `run_prompt.py`
notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md:1797:- runner identity drift: the spec examples still encode `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` while the real records encode `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`
