notes/022_repo_lay_of_the_land__20260416_105729.md:11:This is a primary educational repository for the 3rd edition of "Hands-On Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn, Keras and TensorFlow" (O'Reilly book by Aurélien Géron), containing 19 chapter-structured Jupyter notebooks covering ML fundamentals through reinforcement learning and deployment at scale. 
notes/022_repo_lay_of_the_land__20260416_105729.md:14:- **Primary**: Educational notebooks (stable, well-established)
notes/022_repo_lay_of_the_land__20260416_105729.md:15:- **Active/Secondary**: Codex prompt workflow system (022 task system with execution records, review gates, and incremental architecture maturation)
notes/022_repo_lay_of_the_land__20260416_105729.md:23:- **`images/`** – Organized figure outputs from notebooks, grouped by topic (classification, CNN, RNN, NLP, generative models, RL, deployment, etc.)
notes/022_repo_lay_of_the_land__20260416_105729.md:24:- **`docker/`** – Complete Docker deployment setup with GPU support; includes Dockerfile, Dockerfile.gpu, docker-compose.yml, Makefile for containerized notebook execution
notes/022_repo_lay_of_the_land__20260416_105729.md:33:- **`Weather_Agreement_Lab/`** – Multi-phase weather data experiment: data pull, alignment/cleaning, forecast uncertainty analysis, agreement metrics, data fusion. Includes 5 notebooks, data pipeline, and requirements tracking
notes/022_repo_lay_of_the_land__20260416_105729.md:35:- **`api_practice/`** – Scratch work for API integration patterns; includes API practice notebook and setup notes
notes/022_repo_lay_of_the_land__20260416_105729.md:36:- **`bird_api_demo/`** – Example notebook exploring bird call API usage patterns
notes/022_repo_lay_of_the_land__20260416_105729.md:39:- **`ch04/`** – Deep-dive learning path on gradient descent: "Environment Getting Started" guide, gradient descent from scratch notebook, learning path blueprint, and supporting PDFs
notes/022_repo_lay_of_the_land__20260416_105729.md:55:- **`index.ipynb`** – Landing notebook with navigation links to all chapters and tools
notes/022_repo_lay_of_the_land__20260416_105729.md:59:- **`math_linear_algebra.ipynb`, `math_differential_calculus.ipynb`** – Mathematical foundations notebooks
notes/022_repo_lay_of_the_land__20260416_105729.md:77:   - Clear checkpoint pattern: task execution → record generated → human review gate → next task queued
notes/022_repo_lay_of_the_land__20260416_105729.md:78:   - Recent work (015–022): building queue readiness checker, review backlog helper, v1 record contract validation, doc alignment, and this lay-of-the-land summary
notes/022_repo_lay_of_the_land__20260416_105729.md:83:   - Pair of notebooks and helper/data infrastructure
notes/022_repo_lay_of_the_land__20260416_105729.md:87:**Stability**: Educational notebooks (chapters 01–19) remain stable reference content; the experimental work is isolated in `weather_*/` and `api_practice/` folders.
notes/022_repo_lay_of_the_land__20260416_105729.md:101:7. **Chapter notebooks by topic** – Navigate `index.ipynb`, pick chapters matching your learning interest

notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:135:I updated [Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md), [V1_Execution_Record_Artifact.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Execution_Record_Artifact.md), and [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md). The packet now names `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` as the active V1 runner where applicable, reflects that `review_run.py`, `check_queue_readiness.py`, and `list_review_backlog.py` are implemented parts of the current workflow, and documents the run-id behavior accurately: base identity `<prompt_stem>__<started_at_utc>`, with a numeric suffix like `__2` only when needed for same-second collisions.

notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:901:* a generalized queue engine
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:939:The review gate is the minimum manual checkpoint after execution record creation.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:950:This means the review gate is not a separate platform subsystem.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:961:The review gate defined in `tools/codex/V1_Run_Review_Gate.md` is therefore part of the architecture boundary for V1, not an optional later add-on.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:997:Define the smallest practical review gate between one prompt run finishing and the next prompt in sequence being treated as ready.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1003:- a conservative human stop before queue progression
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1020:- a larger queue state machine
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1066:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1078:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1080:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1082:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1083:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1087:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1098:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1128:The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1135:- automatic queue release
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1137:- separate queue-level state files
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1145:The minimum V1 review gate is:
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1162:Define the smallest change set for [baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py) so the current bridge runner writes a V1 execution record in `notes/` without a broader refactor.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1174:This spec covers only the current bridge runner at [baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py).
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1199:The current gap is that the note conflates execution completion with outcome labeling and does not initialize the V1 review gate.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1274:No broader token accounting, file-diff analysis, validation harvesting, or queue-state calculation should be added in this pass.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1282:- queue progression logic beyond initializing `review_status: UNREVIEWED`
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1313:- runner: `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1409:- `runner` -> literal `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1480:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,260p' tools/codex/run_prompt.py" in /data/git/handson-ml3
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1497:RUNNER_PATH = "tools/codex/run_prompt.py"
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1912:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,260p' tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py" in /data/git/handson-ml3
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:1992:        description="Report whether the next prompt is ready under the current V1 review gate."
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:2446:The V1 prompt workflow is now real, not just specified. The repo has a working runner at `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, an in-place review write-back helper at `tools/codex/review_run.py`, a queue-readiness checker at `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py`, and a review-backlog lister at `tools/codex/list_review_backlog.py`.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:2454:- One bounded prompt run can be executed through `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:2458:- Conservative queue progression is operational through `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py`.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:2472:  - It still targets `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:2473:  - It still names the runner field as `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:2474:  - It still frames review write-back and queue logic as not yet implemented, while the repo now has separate helpers for both.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:2475:- The execution-record spec says the stable V1 `run_id` is `<prompt_stem>__<started_at_utc>`, but `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` now adds same-second suffixes like `__2` when needed. The code is pragmatic; the spec lags.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:2483:The repo has outgrown parts of the design packet. The next prompt or reviewer could still be steered by stale references to `baby_run_prompt.py`, by the old strict run-id rule, or by the older assumption that readiness/backlog support does not exist.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:2493:`run_prompt.py`, `review_run.py`, `check_queue_readiness.py`, and `list_review_backlog.py` all rely on the same markdown field and section conventions. That contract is now central to the workflow, but it is only protected by careful coding and prior notes, not by a small repeatable test layer.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:2496:- repeated required-section and required-field parsing logic in `review_run.py`, `check_queue_readiness.py`, and `list_review_backlog.py`
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:2506:That is not a V1 architecture failure, but it does create friction when interpreting readiness and when deciding whether to trust the queue as a day-to-day operational surface.
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:2509:- `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py` needed a special explanatory `Queue note:`
notes/020_align_v1_doc_and_spec_packet_to_actual_toolset__20260416_010453.md:2512:### 4. Defer retry-linkage tooling and richer queue semantics

notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:182:Implemented the V1 runner candidate in [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py). It keeps the bootstrap runner’s prompt resolution and thin `codex exec` subprocess flow, but now writes a single V1 execution-record markdown artifact with separate execution and review sections, blank review/failure-analysis fields, lightweight resource metrics, stable run-id naming, and numeric suffix collision handling.
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:184:I also wrote the implementation note at [009_run_prompt_candidate_build__20260415_233407.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/009_run_prompt_candidate_build__20260415_233407.md) and validated the runner by invoking it against the smoke-test prompt. That produced [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md), which has the required V1 shape. The `codex exec` subprocess itself exited `1` because session initialization hit `Read-only file system (os error 30)`, so the sample record correctly shows `execution_status: EXECUTION_FAILED`, `review_status: UNREVIEWED`, captured stderr, and the printed record path. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` was not modified.

notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:427:* one manual review gate
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:457:* stopping the queue at `UNREVIEWED`
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:470:* richer queue state machines
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:520:### 4. V1 review gate definition
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:534:No additional required queue file, database, or sidecar should be introduced in V1.
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:552:* a generalized queue engine
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:590:The review gate is the minimum manual checkpoint after execution record creation.
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:601:This means the review gate is not a separate platform subsystem.
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:612:The review gate defined in `tools/codex/V1_Run_Review_Gate.md` is therefore part of the architecture boundary for V1, not an optional later add-on.
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:908:`005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__20260415_202557`
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:915:- it can be created cheaply by the bridge runner
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1123:Define the smallest practical review gate between one prompt run finishing and the next prompt in sequence being treated as ready.
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1129:- a conservative human stop before queue progression
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1146:- a larger queue state machine
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1192:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1204:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1206:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1208:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1209:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1213:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1224:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1254:The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1261:- automatic queue release
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1263:- separate queue-level state files
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1271:The minimum V1 review gate is:
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1288:Define the smallest change set for [baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py) so the current bridge runner writes a V1 execution record in `notes/` without a broader refactor.
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1300:This spec covers only the current bridge runner at [baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py).
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1325:The current gap is that the note conflates execution completion with outcome labeling and does not initialize the V1 review gate.
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:1400:No broader token accounting, file-diff analysis, validation harvesting, or queue-state calculation should be added in this pass.

notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:4960:Implemented the V1 runner candidate in [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py). It keeps the bootstrap runner’s prompt resolution and thin `codex exec` subprocess flow, but now writes a single V1 execution-record markdown artifact with separate execution and review sections, blank review/failure-analysis fields, lightweight resource metrics, stable run-id naming, and numeric suffix collision handling.
notes/009_build_v1_run_prompt_candidate__SUCCESS__20260415_233222.md:4962:I also wrote the implementation note at [009_run_prompt_candidate_build__20260415_233407.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/009_run_prompt_candidate_build__20260415_233407.md) and validated the runner by invoking it against the smoke-test prompt. That produced [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md), which has the required V1 shape. The `codex exec` subprocess itself exited `1` because session initialization hit `Read-only file system (os error 30)`, so the sample record correctly shows `execution_status: EXECUTION_FAILED`, `review_status: UNREVIEWED`, captured stderr, and the printed record path. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` was not modified.

notes/010_run_prompt_candidate_review__20260415_234559.md:5:`tools/codex/run_prompt.py` appears to satisfy the intended V1 bridge-runner role: it executes one prompt, writes one durable execution record in `notes/`, separates execution from review, and stops at `review_status: UNREVIEWED`. The sample record is structurally close to the V1 artifact shape and preserves the failure evidence that matters. The most important next issue is environment-related, not runner design or broader implementation.
notes/010_run_prompt_candidate_review__20260415_234559.md:9:### Does `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` successfully implement the V1 design intent?
notes/010_run_prompt_candidate_review__20260415_234559.md:21:- no queue automation or review automation
notes/010_run_prompt_candidate_review__20260415_234559.md:25:- It stays thin and preserves the bounded behavior of `baby_run_prompt.py` instead of turning into a larger system.
notes/010_run_prompt_candidate_review__20260415_234559.md:36:- The runner identity now correctly points at `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, but `tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md` still describes the prior target as `baby_run_prompt.py`. That is a doc drift issue more than a runner issue.
notes/010_run_prompt_candidate_review__20260415_234559.md:107:- Nothing in `run_prompt.py` suggests it is forcing a read-only mode or attempting forbidden writes beyond the existing thin subprocess path.
notes/001_notebook_finish_report.md:22:- Content: Full chapter-level intro per the treatment template
notes/001_notebook_finish_report.md:55:The body of the notebook is thoroughly treated. Every code cell has a Goal Before that explains what we're about to do and why it matters, and an Implementation Notes After that unpacks the interesting choices. The treatment is consistent in voice, structure, and depth throughout.

notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:619:* one manual review gate
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:649:* stopping the queue at `UNREVIEWED`
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:662:* richer queue state machines
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:712:### 4. V1 review gate definition
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:726:No additional required queue file, database, or sidecar should be introduced in V1.
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:744:* a generalized queue engine
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:782:The review gate is the minimum manual checkpoint after execution record creation.
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:793:This means the review gate is not a separate platform subsystem.
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:804:The review gate defined in `tools/codex/V1_Run_Review_Gate.md` is therefore part of the architecture boundary for V1, not an optional later add-on.

notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:948:`005_define_execution_record_and_generate_next_prompt_queue__20260415_202557`
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:955:- it can be created cheaply by the bridge runner
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1152:Define the smallest practical review gate between one prompt run finishing and the next prompt in sequence being treated as ready.
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1158:- a conservative human stop before queue progression
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1175:- a larger queue state machine
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1221:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1233:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1235:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1237:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1238:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1242:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1253:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1283:The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1290:- automatic queue release
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1292:- separate queue-level state files
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1300:The minimum V1 review gate is:
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1311:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,260p' tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py" in /data/git/handson-ml3
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1387:        description="Report whether the next prompt is ready under the current V1 review gate."
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1575:/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,240p' notes/016_queue_and_backlog_helper_validation__20260416_003710.md" in /data/git/handson-ml3
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1581:I validated `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py` and `tools/codex/list_review_backlog.py` against the current prompt order in `codex_prompts/` and the current V1 execution records in `notes/`.
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1583:Both helpers behaved conservatively and consistently with the V1 design documents. The main visible usability issue is that the default queue-readiness result points back to prompt `002`, because prompts `002` through `010` do not have V1 execution records even though later prompts `011` through `015` do.
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1591:- `notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md`: `EXECUTED`, `UNREVIEWED`
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1592:- `notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md`: `EXECUTED`, `UNREVIEWED`
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1599:The default run of `python3 tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py` selected `codex_prompts/002_repo_inventory_and_status.md` as the target, with `001` as the previous prompt and `notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_234918.md` as the latest prior V1 record. That is correct under the V1 rule set: the latest V1 run for `001` is `ACCEPTED`, and there is no V1 run evidence for `002`, so `002` is the first unreleased prompt in sequence.
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1606:- `--prompt 014`: `Ready: NO`, because latest `013` is `UNREVIEWED` in `notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md`.
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1607:- `--prompt 015`: `Ready: NO`, because latest `014` is `UNREVIEWED` in `notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md`.
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1648:- `check_queue_readiness.py` says the queue is only V1-ready up to prompt `001`, so the next ready prompt is `002`.
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1653:- readiness asks whether the reviewed V1 queue may advance from the start of the prompt sequence
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1660:- readiness also ignores it for queue release because latest `001` is accepted
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1664:The single smallest next improvement is to make `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py` print a short explanation when the default target is blocked by a missing V1 history gap, especially in a repo like this one where prompts `002` through `010` have legacy notes but no V1 execution records.
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1670:- a one-line explanation would reduce confusion without changing queue policy or adding workflow complexity
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1674:- any change to queue rules
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1686:- Add a short gap-explanation line to `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py` when the default target is blocked by missing V1 execution-record history for earlier prompts.
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1691:- A small output clarification would improve operator understanding without changing any queue logic.
notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:1698:- any broader queue engine or dashboard work

notes/017_polish_queue_readiness_gap_explanation__20260416_004355.md:2667:The new output now explains that only V1 execution records in `notes/` count for queue history, that legacy `__SUCCESS__` notes do not, and that this is why the default target can point back to an earlier prompt such as `002`. notes/009_run_prompt_candidate_build__20260415_233407.md:5:Built `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` as a thin V1 runner candidate that resolves one prompt, runs `codex exec` from the repo root, captures `--output-last-message`, captures stderr and return code, writes one V1 execution-record markdown file into `notes/`, prints that path, and exits with the subprocess return code.

notes/004_architecture_and_bridge_runner_review__20260415_195538.md:5:`tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md` is directionally aligned with the governing workflow because it pushes toward smaller parts, clearer ownership, and conservative growth. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` is acceptable as thin bridge tooling for a single bounded slice, but the current architecture doc does not yet translate the workflow into a clear V1 boundary, validation plan, review loop, or failure/resource evidence model. The next step should stay at the design layer: define the minimum execution-record artifact that every run must produce before more tooling is added.
notes/004_architecture_and_bridge_runner_review__20260415_195538.md:26:- The runner does one bounded thing: resolve a prompt, execute `codex exec`, and write a local note. That fits the workflow's allowance for thin bridge tooling that gathers evidence while remaining inspectable and reversible ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:114), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:99)).
notes/004_architecture_and_bridge_runner_review__20260415_195538.md:27:- It validates basic directory prerequisites and fails cleanly when expected folders or prompt files are missing, which is a reasonable minimal safeguard for a bridge script ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:109), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:108)).
notes/004_architecture_and_bridge_runner_review__20260415_195538.md:28:- It writes timestamped markdown notes into `notes/`, which gives the system at least some durable local history of what was run and what Codex returned ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:120), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:46), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:135)).
notes/004_architecture_and_bridge_runner_review__20260415_195538.md:32:- Prompt resolution policy is embedded in the runner. Selection by absolute path, repo-relative path, `codex_prompts/` path, or globbed prefix is helpful now, but prompt identity and lookup rules belong in a prompt-discovery/status layer if the system grows ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:47), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:25)).
notes/004_architecture_and_bridge_runner_review__20260415_195538.md:33:- Note-path and note-content policy are also embedded here. That is acceptable for a bridge script, but note schema, status vocabulary, and execution-record structure should eventually be owned by a note/history module rather than the process launcher ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:96), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:187), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:50)).
notes/004_architecture_and_bridge_runner_review__20260415_195538.md:34:- The runner currently decides final state as `SUCCESS` or `FAILED` based only on the subprocess exit code. That is an expedient bridge behavior, but review judgment, validation outcome, and richer state modeling should not stay inside the launcher if the workflow matures ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:141), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:124)).
notes/004_architecture_and_bridge_runner_review__20260415_195538.md:38:- As bridge tooling it is still acceptable, but it is only partially subordinate to workflow. It captures execution, yet it does not encode the review point that should separate execution from acceptance. A zero exit code becomes `SUCCESS` immediately, which skips the workflow's "validate and inspect before the next step" posture ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:271), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:125)).
notes/004_architecture_and_bridge_runner_review__20260415_195538.md:39:- It stores the original prompt text and final output, but not explicit success criteria, validation evidence, or reviewer judgment. That means the note is an execution artifact, not yet a full workflow artifact ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:251), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).
notes/004_architecture_and_bridge_runner_review__20260415_195538.md:43:- The current setup provides some raw failure evidence because nonzero exit runs still write a note and stderr is preserved when present ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:343), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:128)).
notes/004_architecture_and_bridge_runner_review__20260415_195538.md:49:- The setup preserves only minimal cost evidence today: timestamp in the note filename/content and the captured final output/stderr. That gives some weak signal about artifact volume, but almost nothing about runtime, retries, or service usage ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:382), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:17), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).

notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:578:- it can be created cheaply by the bridge runner
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:829:* one manual review gate
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:859:* stopping the queue at `UNREVIEWED`
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:872:* richer queue state machines
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:922:### 4. V1 review gate definition
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:936:No additional required queue file, database, or sidecar should be introduced in V1.
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:954:* a generalized queue engine
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:992:The review gate is the minimum manual checkpoint after execution record creation.
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1003:This means the review gate is not a separate platform subsystem.
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1014:The review gate defined in `tools/codex/V1_Run_Review_Gate.md` is therefore part of the architecture boundary for V1, not an optional later add-on.
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1050:Define the smallest practical review gate between one prompt run finishing and the next prompt in sequence being treated as ready.
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1056:- a conservative human stop before queue progression
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1073:- a larger queue state machine
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1119:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1131:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1133:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1135:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1136:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1140:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1151:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1181:The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1188:- automatic queue release
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1190:- separate queue-level state files
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1198:The minimum V1 review gate is:
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1217:Define the smallest change set for [baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py) so the current bridge runner writes a V1 execution record in `notes/` without a broader refactor.
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1229:This spec covers only the current bridge runner at [baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py).
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1254:The current gap is that the note conflates execution completion with outcome labeling and does not initialize the V1 review gate.
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1329:No broader token accounting, file-diff analysis, validation harvesting, or queue-state calculation should be added in this pass.
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1337:- queue progression logic beyond initializing `review_status: UNREVIEWED`
notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:1368:- runner: `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`

notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:2346:+`tools/codex/run_prompt.py` appears to satisfy the intended V1 bridge-runner role: it executes one prompt, writes one durable execution record in `notes/`, separates execution from review, and stops at `review_status: UNREVIEWED`. notes/010_review_run_prompt_candidate_and_recommend_next_step__SUCCESS__20260415_234523.md:2647:+`tools/codex/run_prompt.py` appears to satisfy the intended V1 bridge-runner role: it executes one prompt, writes one durable execution record in `notes/`, separates execution from review, and stops at `review_status: UNREVIEWED`. The sample record is structurally close to the V1 artifact shape and preserves the failure evidence that matters. The most important next issue is environment-relaotes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:6931:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1484:            return run_id, candidate

notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:6941:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1869:- Resource and failure evidence capture exists in the actual record body. The runner records `return_code`, `elapsed_seconds`, output/stderr character counts, and full stderr text when present. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:144) and the failed record [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md:8).
notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:6942:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1871:- The failure path is demonstrated, not hypothetical. The failed smoke-test run preserved the read-only/session-start stderr cleanly while still producing a reviewable record. See [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md:83).
notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:6943:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1878:- Review is operationally usable now. The accepted smoke-test record shows the exact intended lifecycle: executed record first, then in-place write-back to `ACCEPTED`. See [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_234918.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_234918.md:16).
notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:6944:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1909:- the current review backlog
notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:6945:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1915:The execution-record artifact says the stable V1 `run_id` is `<prompt_stem>__<started_at_utc>`, but `run_prompt.py` adds `__2`, `__3`, etc. on same-second collisions. See [V1_Execution_Record_Artifact.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Execution_Record_Artifact.md:37) versus [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:59).
notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:6946:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1921:- `tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md` still targets `baby_run_prompt.py` as the implementation target and still names the runner as `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`. See [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:5), [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:17), [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:156), and [V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/V1_Bridge_Runner_Change_Spec.md:252).
notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:6947:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1937:The record shape includes `retry_of_run_id` and manual failure-analysis fields, but the current tools do not help a reviewer connect a rejected run to a retry decision or derive the next retry target.
notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:6948:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:1941:The failed smoke-test run shows that Codex session startup can fail before task work begins. That is not a runner-design flaw, but it does mean the workflow still benefits from a small amount of environment-oriented diagnosis or runbook support when repeated use hits startup failures. See [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md:83).

notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:7050:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:2806:The review concludes the V1 pipe is now a real usable thin slice: `run_prompt.py` creates durable execution records, `review_run.py` performs in-place manual review write-back, and the main remaining weakness is operational support for repeated use rather than missing architecture. The top recommendation is to add a small queue-readiness checker next, followed by an unreviewed-run lister and then lightweight contract validation for the two V1 scripts.

notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:7197:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:5248:One implementation question is left explicit: whether to keep second-precision timestamps as-is and fail on same-second collisions, or add a minimal collision suffix. notes/019_generate_prompts_for_doc_alignment_and_record_contract_validation__20260416_010209.md:7469:notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md:10213:I found the last false positive source: real V1 records keep `execution_status` and `review_status` after the first section break, while legacy notes don’t carry the V1 `run_id` header at all. I’m switching the parser to use the header only to decide “is this a V1 record candidate?”, then reading the full metadata block up to `## Prompt Text` for the required fields.

notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:167:The report classifies `notes/` and `codex_prompts/` into the four requested buckets, with file-specific reasons. The main conservative call is that current V1 execution records remain `uncertain` because `check_queue_readiness.py` and `list_review_backlog.py` still treat them as live source-of-truth data, so moving them now would change behavior. No files were moved or deleted.

notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:389:* stopping the queue at `UNREVIEWED`
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:402:* richer queue state machines
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:452:### 4. V1 review gate definition
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:466:No additional required queue file, database, or sidecar should be introduced in V1.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:472:* `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` for bounded execution and execution-record creation
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:474:* `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py` for conservative next-prompt readiness checks
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:496:* a generalized queue engine
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:534:The review gate is the minimum manual checkpoint after execution record creation.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:545:This means the review gate is not a separate platform subsystem.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:556:The review gate defined in `tools/codex/V1_Run_Review_Gate.md` is therefore part of the architecture boundary for V1, not an optional later add-on.

notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:937:`tools/codex/run_prompt.py` uses that base identity and adds a numeric suffix such as `__2` only when needed to avoid a same-second filename collision for the same prompt.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1112:Define the smallest practical review gate between one prompt run finishing and the next prompt in sequence being treated as ready.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1118:- a conservative human stop before queue progression
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1135:- a larger queue state machine
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1181:- `UNREVIEWED`: default state after execution record creation; queue must stop here.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1193:- `UNREVIEWED` stops the queue pending manual review.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1195:- `REJECTED` stops the queue and forces an explicit human decision about what happens next.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1197:`EXECUTED` does not release the queue.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1198:`EXECUTION_FAILED` does not itself decide the queue either, but it will usually lead to `REJECTED` on review.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1202:`REJECTED` is the V1 outcome that stops the queue and forces a new design or retry decision.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1213:V1 only requires that the queue does not continue past a rejected run.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:1243:The V1 review gate is therefore not a new artifact.

notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4925:`tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md` is directionally aligned with the governing workflow because it pushes toward smaller parts, clearer ownership, and conservative growth. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` is acceptable as thin bridge tooling for a single bounded slice, but the current architecture doc does not yet translate the workflow into a clear V1 boundary, validation plan, review loop, or failure/resource evidence model. The next step should stay at the design layer: define the minimum execution-record artifact that every run must produce before more tooling is added.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4946:- The runner does one bounded thing: resolve a prompt, execute `codex exec`, and write a local note. That fits the workflow's allowance for thin bridge tooling that gathers evidence while remaining inspectable and reversible ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:114), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:99)).
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4947:- It validates basic directory prerequisites and fails cleanly when expected folders or prompt files are missing, which is a reasonable minimal safeguard for a bridge script ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:109), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:108)).
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4948:- It writes timestamped markdown notes into `notes/`, which gives the system at least some durable local history of what was run and what Codex returned ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:120), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:46), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:135)).
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4952:- Prompt resolution policy is embedded in the runner. Selection by absolute path, repo-relative path, `codex_prompts/` path, or globbed prefix is helpful now, but prompt identity and lookup rules belong in a prompt-discovery/status layer if the system grows ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:47), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:25)).
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4953:- Note-path and note-content policy are also embedded here. That is acceptable for a bridge script, but note schema, status vocabulary, and execution-record structure should eventually be owned by a note/history module rather than the process launcher ([tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:96), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:187), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:50)).
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4954:- The runner currently decides final state as `SUCCESS` or `FAILED` based only on the subprocess exit code. That is an expedient bridge behavior, but review judgment, validation outcome, and richer state modeling should not stay inside the launcher if the workflow matures ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md:141), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:124)).
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4958:- As bridge tooling it is still acceptable, but it is only partially subordinate to workflow. It captures execution, yet it does not encode the review point that should separate execution from acceptance. A zero exit code becomes `SUCCESS` immediately, which skips the workflow's "validate and inspect before the next step" posture ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:104), [tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:271), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:125)).
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4959:- It stores the original prompt text and final output, but not explicit success criteria, validation evidence, or reviewer judgment. That means the note is an execution artifact, not yet a full workflow artifact ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:251), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4963:- The current setup provides some raw failure evidence because nonzero exit runs still write a note and stderr is preserved when present ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:343), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:128)).
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:4969:- The setup preserves only minimal cost evidence today: timestamp in the note filename/content and the captured final output/stderr. That gives some weak signal about artifact volume, but almost nothing about runtime, retries, or service usage ([tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/Project_Design_Workflow.md:382), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:17), [tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py:58)).

notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:6548:Implemented the V1 runner candidate in [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py). It keeps the bootstrap runner’s prompt resolution and thin `codex exec` subprocess flow, but now writes a single V1 execution-record markdown artifact with separate execution and review sections, blank review/failure-analysis fields, lightweight resource metrics, stable run-id naming, and numeric suffix collision handling.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:6550:I also wrote the implementation note at [009_run_prompt_candidate_build__20260415_233407.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/009_run_prompt_candidate_build__20260415_233407.md) and validated the runner by invoking it against the smoke-test prompt. That produced [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md), which has the required V1 shape. The `codex exec` subprocess itself exited `1` because session initialization hit `Read-only file system (os error 30)`, so the sample record correctly shows `execution_status: EXECUTION_FAILED`, `review_status: UNREVIEWED`, captured stderr, and the printed record path. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` was not modified.

notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:6984:`tools/codex/run_prompt.py` appears to satisfy the intended V1 bridge-runner role: it executes one prompt, writes one durable execution record in `notes/`, separates execution from review, and stops at `review_status: UNREVIEWED`. The sample record is structurally close to the V1 artifact shape and preserves the failure evidence that matters. The most important next issue is environment-related, not runner design or broader implementation.

notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7642:The review concludes the V1 pipe is now a real usable thin slice: `run_prompt.py` creates durable execution records, `review_run.py` performs in-place manual review write-back, and the main remaining weakness is operational support for repeated use rather than missing architecture. The top recommendation is to add a small queue-readiness checker next, followed by an unreviewed-run lister and then lightweight contract validation for the two V1 scripts.

notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7789:- Prompt resolution exists and is conservative. `run_prompt.py` preserves the same direct-path, `codex_prompts/`, and unique-prefix lookup behavior as the bridge runner baseline. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:31).
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7790:- Codex invocation is still thin and inspectable. The runner shells out through `codex exec -C <repo_root> --output-last-message <tempfile> -` and captures return code, final output, and stderr. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:74).
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7791:- Execution-record creation exists and matches the intended V1 section order. The runner writes header, execution facts, review facts, failure analysis, resource facts, prompt text, final output, and stderr into one markdown file. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:97).
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7792:- Resource and failure evidence capture exists in the actual record body. The runner records `return_code`, `elapsed_seconds`, output/stderr character counts, and full stderr text when present. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:144) and the failed record [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md:8).
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7793:- The separation between execution outcome and acceptance outcome is implemented correctly. New records start at `review_status: UNREVIEWED` regardless of `EXECUTED` vs `EXECUTION_FAILED`. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:130).
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7800:- The helper is consistent with the V1 review gate. It only allows `ACCEPTED` or `REJECTED`, auto-fills `reviewed_at_utc`, and limits failure-analysis updates to rejected runs. See [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:12) and [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py:148).
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7816:### 1. Conservative queue progression is still architectural, not operational
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:7838:The execution-record artifact says the stable V1 `run_id` is `<prompt_stem>__<started_at_utc>`, but `run_prompt.py` adds `__2`, `__3`, etc. on same-second collisions. notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:8918:===== notes/016_queue_and_backlog_helper_validation__20260416_003710.md =====
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:8923:I validated `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py` and `tools/codex/list_review_backlog.py` against the current prompt order in `codex_prompts/` and the current V1 execution records in `notes/`.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:8925:Both helpers behaved conservatively and consistently with the V1 design documents. The main visible usability issue is that the default queue-readiness result points back to prompt `002`, because prompts `002` through `010` do not have V1 execution records even though later prompts `011` through `015` do.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:8933:- `notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md`: `EXECUTED`, `UNREVIEWED`
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:8934:- `notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md`: `EXECUTED`, `UNREVIEWED`
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:8941:The default run of `python3 tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py` selected `codex_prompts/002_repo_inventory_and_status.md` as the target, with `001` as the previous prompt and `notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_234918.md` as the latest prior V1 record. That is correct under the V1 rule set: the latest V1 run for `001` is `ACCEPTED`, and there is no V1 run evidence for `002`, so `002` is the first unreleased prompt in sequence.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:8948:- `--prompt 014`: `Ready: NO`, because latest `013` is `UNREVIEWED` in `notes/013_generate_prompts_for_queue_readiness_and_review_backlog_helpers__20260416_001937.md`.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:8949:- `--prompt 015`: `Ready: NO`, because latest `014` is `UNREVIEWED` in `notes/014_build_queue_readiness_checker__20260416_002319.md`.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:8990:- `check_queue_readiness.py` says the queue is only V1-ready up to prompt `001`, so the next ready prompt is `002`.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:8995:- readiness asks whether the reviewed V1 queue may advance from the start of the prompt sequence
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9002:- readiness also ignores it for queue release because latest `001` is accepted
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9006:The single smallest next improvement is to make `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py` print a short explanation when the default target is blocked by a missing V1 history gap, especially in a repo like this one where prompts `002` through `010` have legacy notes but no V1 execution records.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9012:- a one-line explanation would reduce confusion without changing queue policy or adding workflow complexity
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9016:- any change to queue rules

notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9211:Validation result: both helpers behave correctly and conservatively against the current repo state. `check_queue_readiness.py` correctly treats `002` as the next ready prompt because only `001` has a latest `ACCEPTED` V1 record, and `list_review_backlog.py` correctly distinguishes the stale unreviewed failed `001` run from the newer accepted `001` run by keeping it in the all-unreviewed list but excluding it from latest-per-prompt and likely-needs-review-next.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9213:The single recommended next step is a small output clarification in `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py` so it explains missing V1 history gaps for earlier prompts like `002` through `010`, which currently have legacy notes but no V1 execution records.

notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9467:The new output now explains that only V1 execution records in `notes/` count for queue history, that legacy `__SUCCESS__` notes do not, and that this is why the default target can point back to an earlier prompt such as `002`. notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9592:   `Queue note: default selection uses only V1 execution records in notes/. Legacy __SUCCESS__ notes do not count as V1 queue history, so prompts 002, 003, 004, 005, 006, 007, 008, 009, 010 still count as missing V1 evidence and can pull the default target earlier than older notes suggest.`

notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9612:The V1 prompt workflow is now real, not just specified. The repo has a working runner at `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, an in-place review write-back helper at `tools/codex/review_run.py`, a queue-readiness checker at `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py`, and a review-backlog lister at `tools/codex/list_review_backlog.py`.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9620:- One bounded prompt run can be executed through `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9624:- Conservative queue progression is operational through `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py`.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9638:  - It still targets `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9639:  - It still names the runner field as `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9640:  - It still frames review write-back and queue logic as not yet implemented, while the repo now has separate helpers for both.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9641:- The execution-record spec says the stable V1 `run_id` is `<prompt_stem>__<started_at_utc>`, but `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` now adds same-second suffixes like `__2` when needed. The code is pragmatic; the spec lags.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9649:The repo has outgrown parts of the design packet. The next prompt or reviewer could still be steered by stale references to `baby_run_prompt.py`, by the old strict run-id rule, or by the older assumption that readiness/backlog support does not exist.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9659:`run_prompt.py`, `review_run.py`, `check_queue_readiness.py`, and `list_review_backlog.py` all rely on the same markdown field and section conventions. That contract is now central to the workflow, but it is only protected by careful coding and prior notes, not by a small repeatable test layer.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9662:- repeated required-section and required-field parsing logic in `review_run.py`, `check_queue_readiness.py`, and `list_review_backlog.py`
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9672:That is not a V1 architecture failure, but it does create friction when interpreting readiness and when deciding whether to trust the queue as a day-to-day operational surface.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9675:- `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py` needed a special explanatory `Queue note:`
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:9678:### 4. Defer retry-linkage tooling and richer queue semantics

notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:10918:  `tools/Codex_Prompt_Workflow_Architecture.md` is directionally aligned with the governing workflow because it pushes toward smaller parts, clearer ownership, and conservative growth. `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py` is acceptable as thin bridge tooling for a single bounded slice, but the current architecture doc does not yet translate the workflow into a clear V1 boundary, validation plan, review loop, or failure/resource evidence model. The next step should stay at the design layer: define the minimum execution-record artifact that every run must produce before more tooling is added.

notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:11215:  `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` appears to satisfy the intended V1 bridge-runner role: it executes one prompt, writes one durable execution record in `notes/`, separates execution from review, and stops at `review_status: UNREVIEWED`. The sample record is structurally close to the V1 artifact shape and preserves the failure evidence that matters. The most important next issue is environment-related, not runner design or broader implementation.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:11219:  ### Does `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` successfully implement the V1 design intent?
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:11231:  - no queue automation or review automation
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:11248:  - runner: `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:11300:  - runner: `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:11319:  ### 1. Add a queue-readiness checker
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:11335:  ### 3. Add lightweight contract validation for `run_prompt.py` and `review_run.py`
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:11349:  - execute one prompt through `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:11360:  - Prompt resolution exists and is conservative. `run_prompt.py` preserves the same direct-path, `codex_prompts/`, and unique-prefix lookup behavior as the bridge runner baseline. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:31).
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:11361:  - Codex invocation is still thin and inspectable. The runner shells out through `codex exec -C <repo_root> --output-last-message <tempfile> -` and captures return code, final output, and stderr. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:74).
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:11362:  - Execution-record creation exists and matches the intended V1 section order. The runner writes header, execution facts, review facts, failure analysis, resource facts, prompt text, final output, and stderr into one markdown file. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:97).
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:11363:  - Resource and failure evidence capture exists in the actual record body. The runner records `return_code`, `elapsed_seconds`, output/stderr character counts, and full stderr text when present. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:144) and the failed record [001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md](/data/git/handson-ml3/notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_233343.md:8).
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:11364:  - The separation between execution outcome and acceptance outcome is implemented correctly. New records start at `review_status: UNREVIEWED` regardless of `EXECUTED` vs `EXECUTION_FAILED`. See [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py:130).

The main visible usability issue is that the default queue-readiness result points back to prompt `002`, because prompts `002` through `010` do not have V1 execution records even though later prompts `011` through `015` do.

notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:11554:  The default run of `python3 tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py` selected `codex_prompts/002_repo_inventory_and_status.md` as the target, with `001` as the previous prompt and `notes/001_smoke_test_pipeline__20260415_234918.md` as the latest prior V1 record. That is correct under the V1 rule set: the latest V1 run for `001` is `ACCEPTED`, and there is no V1 run evidence for `002`, so `002` is the first unreleased prompt in sequence.

The repo has a working runner at `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, an in-place review write-back helper at `tools/codex/review_run.py`, a queue-readiness checker at `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py`, and a review-backlog lister at `tools/codex/list_review_backlog.py`.

notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:12335:The review concludes the V1 pipe is now a real usable thin slice: `run_prompt.py` creates durable execution records, `review_run.py` performs in-place manual review write-back, and the main remaining weakness is operational support for repeated use rather than missing architecture. The top recommendation is to add a small queue-readiness checker next, followed by an unreviewed-run lister and then lightweight contract validation for the two V1 scripts.

notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:12565:The V1 prompt workflow is now real, not just specified. The repo has a working runner at `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`, an in-place review write-back helper at `tools/codex/review_run.py`, a queue-readiness checker at `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py`, and a review-backlog lister at `tools/codex/list_review_backlog.py`.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:12573:- One bounded prompt run can be executed through `tools/codex/run_prompt.py`.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:12577:- Conservative queue progression is operational through `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py`.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:12591:  - It still targets `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:12592:  - It still names the runner field as `tools/codex/baby_run_prompt.py`.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:12593:  - It still frames review write-back and queue logic as not yet implemented, while the repo now has separate helpers for both.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:12594:- The execution-record spec says the stable V1 `run_id` is `<prompt_stem>__<started_at_utc>`, but `tools/codex/run_prompt.py` now adds same-second suffixes like `__2` when needed. The code is pragmatic; the spec lags.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:12602:The repo has outgrown parts of the design packet. The next prompt or reviewer could still be steered by stale references to `baby_run_prompt.py`, by the old strict run-id rule, or by the older assumption that readiness/backlog support does not exist.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:12612:`run_prompt.py`, `review_run.py`, `check_queue_readiness.py`, and `list_review_backlog.py` all rely on the same markdown field and section conventions. That contract is now central to the workflow, but it is only protected by careful coding and prior notes, not by a small repeatable test layer.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:12615:- repeated required-section and required-field parsing logic in `review_run.py`, `check_queue_readiness.py`, and `list_review_backlog.py`
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:12625:That is not a V1 architecture failure, but it does create friction when interpreting readiness and when deciding whether to trust the queue as a day-to-day operational surface.
notes/022_audit_and_classify_scaffolding_for_summary_and_attic__20260416_022902.md:12628:- `tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py` needed a special explanatory `Queue note:`

notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:108:4. Confirm `review_run.py`, `check_queue_readiness.py`, and `list_review_backlog.py` still work with the shared validator in place.
notes/021_add_lightweight_v1_record_contract_validation__20260416_010810.md:124:Implemented a shared V1 record validator in [v1_record_validation.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/v1_record_validation.py) and rewired [run_prompt.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/run_prompt.py), [review_run.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/review_run.py), [check_queue_readiness.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/check_queue_readiness.py), and [list_review_backlog.py](/data/git/handson-ml3/tools/codex/list_review_backlog.py) to reuse that one path instead of carrying separate markdown-contract checks. The shared path now enforces required section order, required field lines, allowed status values, the `run_id` pattern with optional same-second suffix, and basic identity consistency such as title/run-id and prompt-file/prompt-stem agreement.
